calendar.matters:
SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Jun-19-2008 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: 9:05am
Cause called for Small Claims appeals. Judge Pro Tem Glen Lerner, Judge presiding, clerk-Beth Wilber, nothing on record.
Appellant, Jonathan Jew, present through counsel, Albert W. Lee, Esq., Also present are Mr. Tang's parents; Respondent, Jason Tang (minor) is present in Pro Per, with mother: Mei Hao Liang, and sister: Julia Tang. Julia Tang will serve as his Cantonese interpreter and present the case in Pro Per. Parties and witnesses are sworn.
Both parties lodge trial briefs that will be filed and entered. The court takes a recess to allow all the Court and the parties to read the briefs.
9:25am
Court resumes. Mr. Lee states that Mr. Jew was never properly served. The court reviews the proof of service and finds that the Court does have jurisdiction and the case will proceed.
9:40am
Ms. Tang calls JASON TANG as a witness an proceeds with questing.
10:03am
Mr. Lee begins cross-examination
10:11am
Re-direct. The judge has questions for the witness, and then excuses Mr. Tang.
10:15am
Ms. Tang calls JONATHAN JEW and begins direct examination. Judge Lerner allows Mr. Jew to testify in the narrative, and has questions for him during testimony.
EXHR(A) Revised medical expenses
EXHR(B) letter requesting medical reimbursement
10:45am
Court takes a recess
EXHR(C) Medical records, marked collectively
10:57am
Court reconvenes, all present. Mr. Lee requests that Ms. Tang respond to questions re authenticity of the medical records. The Court and parties review the medical records.
Mr. Lee then calls guardian ad litem, MIE HAO LIANG, and Julia Tang acts as interpreter.
11:30am
No further questions. The Judge has questions for Ms. Tang re the medical bills.
EXHR(D) Medical consultation response
11:38am
Respondent rests. Ms. Tang gives closing statements
11:45am
Mr. Lee presents arguments on behalf of the appellant.
1:50am
Rebuttal by Ms. Tang
11:51am
No further matters are before the court. Matter is submitted. Evidence is retained.

SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Apr-24-2008 Continued To Jun-18-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 525

PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM
Off Calendar Feb-01-2008 Judgment Entered

PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM
Off Calendar Oct-18-2007 Reset From Oct-30-07 To Nov-29-07 At 03:30 P.M. In Dept 506
